govukComponents.usage: Section caption "Description of the goods" above the H1
Sub-headings: "Commodities", per-commodity description heading, "Total for this consignment"
Commodities overview table + per-commodity species/weights/packages editable table
Package type picker per species row
Net weight, Number of packages (per species row) and Total gross weight text inputs
Update total (secondary), Save and return to hub, Save and continue
Consignment totals: Net weight (kg/units) and Number of packages
Table column labels + Total gross weight label
Visible horizontal rules separating the commodities / species / totals sections
JS-driven history back link in the service header region

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: commodity-detail-form-desktop / commodity-detail-form-mobile + commodities-table-desktop / commodities-table-mobile + species-table-desktop / species-table-mobile + table-responsive + js-hidden
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: The entire commodity form is rendered TWICE — a desktop variant and a mobile variant — with duplicate input names (e.g. two inputs named 03019230-1756325.net-weight, one id ...-desktop one id ...-mobile). One variant is hidden via js-hidden depending on viewport. This is a bespoke responsive-table strategy.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: Duplicated form controls with identical name attributes is fragile, doubles the DOM, and depends on client JS to hide one copy; a no-JS user could submit ambiguous data. The new app should render each field ONCE and rely on standard responsive CSS / the govuk Table's own responsive behaviour instead of duplicating the form.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: A single govuk Table with responsive styling, or restructure weights/packages capture as one govuk form per species (govuk-form-group per field) rather than an editable grid.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 33 — controls.txt shows -desktop and -mobile duplicate inputs; classes include commodity-detail-form-desktop/mobile, table-responsive, js-hidden. Corroborated by page-object CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage.ts:88-106 — the CHED-D locators (.commodity-detail-form-desktop input.net-weight, #gross-weight-desktop, save-and-continue-desktop) confirm the desktop/mobile dual-form is a shared pattern, not a one-off.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: "Add commodity" and "Add species" are <button> elements styled with a custom link-button class (rendered to look like text links, not govuk buttons).
nonStandardPatterns.concern: A custom class instead of either a real govuk Button or a real link blurs the semantic/visual contract. In the rebuild these are add-another actions.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk Button (govuk-button--secondary) for the action, or the GOV.UK 'Add another' pattern if the new app keeps an editable list of species/commodities.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 33 — structure.txt BUTTON.link-button 'Add species' / 'Add commodity'. Page-object CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage.ts:34 (btnAddCommodity, role=button name 'Add commodity').

nonStandardPatterns.pattern: species-table-cheda, commodity-overview, subtotal, subheader, net-weight, number-of-packages, type-of-package, remove, species, border-bottom-none
nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: IPAFFS-specific styling/behaviour hooks bolted onto govuk-table cells and controls (subtotal row, per-column widths, remove-row action, CHED-A-shared table class 'species-table-cheda').
nonStandardPatterns.concern: These are app-specific CSS/JS hooks, not Design System classes. The 'species-table-cheda' name shows the table markup is shared across CHED types — the new standalone IUU app should model its own weights/packages capture cleanly rather than inheriting the shared editable-grid widget.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: govuk Table for read-only display; standard govuk-form-group fields for the editable net weight / number of packages / package type; a separate 'remove' link per row (GOV.UK add-another pattern).
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 33 — nonstd.txt. Corroborated: the single shared page-object CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage.ts exposes controls for CHED-A (inputNumberOfAnimals :14), CHED-P/IUU (inputNetWeight :6, inputNumberOfPackages :10), CHED-PP (inputNetWeightinKgs :47, inputQuantity :59, dropdownQuantityType :63, dropdownControlledAtmosphereContainer :67, dropdownIntendedUse :71, checkboxTestAndTrial :75) and CHED-D (:88-106) — one template drives all four CHED types.

nonStandardPatterns.whatItDoes: A visually/js-hidden helper submit button included so pressing Enter in a text field submits the intended form.
nonStandardPatterns.concern: A workaround for the multi-form / editable-grid layout. Not needed with a conventional single-form GOV.UK page.
nonStandardPatterns.govukAlternative: Standard single <form> with one primary govuk Button — native Enter-to-submit needs no helper.
nonStandardPatterns.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 33 — structure.txt BUTTON.js-hidden 'Keyboard submit'

fields.name: {commodityCode}-{lineId}.net-weight (observed: 03019230-1756325.net-weight)
fields.label: Net weight (kg/units)
fields.conditionalOn: one per species row within a commodity (repeats when species are added)
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 33 — textbox 'Net weight for Anguilla spp. 1-1', input name 03019230-1756325.net-weight. Corroborated by page-object CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage.ts:6-8 (inputNetWeight, role=textbox name 'Net weight') and always filled by the IUU fish workflow ched-p-workflows.ts:889 (netWeight '1'). Client-side 'required' attr absent (DOM required=false); the workflow fills it on every run with no optional branch, so it is de-facto mandatory for submission (server-side enforced) — see openQuestions.

fields.name: {commodityCode}-{lineId}.num-packages (observed: 03019230-1756325.num-packages)
fields.label: Number of packages
fields.conditionalOn: one per species row within a commodity (repeats when species are added)
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 33 — textbox 'Number of packages for Anguilla spp. 1-1', input name 03019230-1756325.num-packages. Corroborated by page-object CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage.ts:10-12 (inputNumberOfPackages) and always filled by the IUU fish workflow ched-p-workflows.ts:890 (numberOfPackages '2'). DOM required=false; always filled by the workflow → de-facto mandatory server-side (see openQuestions).

fields.name: {commodityCode}-{lineId}.package-type (observed: 03019230-1756325.package-type)
fields.label: Type of package
fields.conditionalOn: one per species row within a commodity (repeats when species are added)
options: Container, not otherwise specified as transport equipment
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 33 — combobox 'Package type for Anguilla spp. 1-1', select name 03019230-1756325.package-type; full option list captured (26 real package types + 'Select type of package' placeholder). Corroborated by page-object CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage.ts:18-20 (dropdownPackageType, getByLabel 'Package type') and workflow ched-p-workflows.ts:891 (packageType.box). DISCREPANCY: the QA domain catalogue types/package-type.ts lists 33 package types (verified: 33 keyed entries, package-type.ts:1-34; adds these 7 over the rendered 26 — 'Bottle, flask and other glass packages', 'Bulk solid granular particles ("grains")', 'Container' [short form], 'Wood bundle', 'Wood crate', 'Wooden barrel', 'Wooden case with pallet base'); the IUU page rendered only 26 for this commodity (26 + 7 = 33). The rendered 26 wins (confirmed); the wider catalogue is reference data whose visible subset is commodity/CHED-type driven — see openQuestions.

fields.name: gross-weight
fields.label: Total gross weight (kg/units)
fields.evidence: trace db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d action 33 — textbox 'Total gross weight (kg/units)', input name gross-weight; action 34 directly records Fill "22" into the resolved gross-weight-desktop control. Corroborated by page-object CommodityExtendedDescriptionPage.ts:26-28 (inputTotalGrossWeight, role=textbox name /Total gross weight/) and IUU fish workflow ched-p-workflows.ts:892. DOM required=false; always filled by the workflow → de-facto mandatory server-side (see openQuestions).

validationMessages.field: net weight / number of packages / package type / total gross weight
validationMessages.trigger: Submitting the commodity details page with missing or non-numeric weight/package values
validationMessages.evidence: No error-state snapshot for this page was found in the evidence trace (0 errors on db2d277c5c8bfcf2dc0f2278bc92dc7aa8fdf42d), and NO CHED-P test asserts any validation copy for this page — every CHED-P/IUU spec and the accessibility test only fill valid values and click Save and continue (ched-p-workflows.ts:889-893; ched-p-accessibility-tests.spec.ts:73-78; ched-p-notification.spec.ts fish flow asserts only downstream review/confirmation, never a weights/packages error). Verbatim validation copy is therefore neither observed nor asserted — needs an error-bearing IUU/CHED-P trace or (out of scope here) the IPAFFS commodity-details template to confirm.

openQuestions: Validation copy is a GAP: the evidence trace is a happy-path (0 errors) and no test asserts any error string for this page. Need an error-bearing trace to capture verbatim error messages for net weight, number of packages, package type and total gross weight (required? numeric? must-be-positive? kg vs units?).

The page (H1 'Commodity', title 'Commodity details', URL .../commodity/details) is a COMBINED page that also carries the commodities table + Add commodity + Add species. The 'commodity-extended-description' slug (net weight/packages/package type/gross weight) is one section of this larger page — confirm the new IUU app should split commodity selection from the weights/packages capture, or keep them on one page.

'required' is reported false on every input in the DOM (no client-side required attribute); required-ness is enforced server-side. The IUU fish workflow (ched-p-workflows.ts:889-893) fills ALL FOUR fields on every run with no optional branch, and the ChedPNotificationConfig marks netWeight/numberOfPackages/packageType/totalGrossWeight as always-populated defaults — strongly implying all four are mandatory for IUU submission. Confirm with the business.

Net weight and Number of packages are per-SPECIES per-COMMODITY (repeating grid); Total gross weight is per-consignment. Confirm the new app's data model captures weights/packages at the species-line level and gross weight at consignment level.

The label reads 'Net weight (kg/units)' — units are ambiguous (kg OR units depending on commodity). CHED-PP uses 'Net weight (kg)' (page-object :47) and a separate Quantity + quantity-type pair (:59-65); CHED-A uses 'Number of animals' (:14). Confirm whether IUU fishery products are captured in kg, units, or both, and whether the unit is derived from the commodity code.

The package-type list rendered 26 values for this commodity, but the QA domain catalogue types/package-type.ts holds 33 (7 extra: Bottle/flask glass packages, Bulk solid granular particles, Container [short], Wood bundle, Wood crate, Wooden barrel, Wooden case with pallet base). Confirm the visible subset is commodity/CHED-type driven reference data and that the IUU app sources the package-type list from the reference-data service rather than hardcoding either the 26 or the 33.

The shared page object exposes a 'Select all' checkbox (page-object :30) + an 'Apply' button (:38) — a bulk-apply widget (set one package-type/weight across all selected species rows) that NO IUU trace in our corpus rendered (single-species journeys only). Confirm whether IUU needs bulk-apply for multi-species catches, or whether per-row entry suffices.

The single page object drives all four CHED types (CHED-A number-of-animals, CHED-P/IUU net-weight, CHED-PP net-weight-kg + quantity + quantity-type + controlled-atmosphere + intended-use + test-and-trial, CHED-D desktop/mobile dual-form). The standalone IUU app should model ONLY the fish/IUU fields (net weight kg/units, number of packages, package type, total gross weight) and must not inherit the plant/animal/feed variant controls.
